title: DID document endpoint has no access control — exposes account existence and public keys
description:
The /users/{username}/did.json endpoint (activitypub.rs:805-852) has no authentication or rate limiting. This is technically correct behavior for a did:web endpoint (DID documents are public by design), but worth noting:

1. The endpoint exposes whether a username exists (404 vs 200), same as the actor endpoint — this is expected for ActivityPub servers
2. The RSA public key and Ed25519 recovery public key are intentionally public — these are verification keys, not secrets
3. No rate limiting, but this applies to all routes equally (not DID-specific)

VERDICT: No action needed. DID documents are public by specification. The endpoint correctly serves only public key material. This is a non-finding, filed for audit completeness.

Affected: src/activitypub.rs:805-852
close_reason: Non-finding. DID documents are public by specification.
